title: First Solar Revises 2025 Guidance Down Due to Tariff Impact
text: First Solar has significantly reduced its 2025 financial forecasts in response to Trump administration tariffs. The company lowered its net sales guidance from $5.3-5.8 billion to $4.5-5.5 billion, and module sales expectations from 18-20GW to 15.5-19.3GW. The CdTe thin-film manufacturer cited the 10% universal tariff as a primary concern. CEO Mark Widmar explained the meaningful adverse gross margin impact to sales into the US, absent the duty being fully passed through to the module buyer. First Solar operates facilities in India, Malaysia, and Vietnam. The company anticipates its Indian production will pivot toward domestic markets rather than U.S. exports. For Southeast Asian operations, management stated they may need to further reduce or idle production at one or both of these locations. During Q1 2025, First Solar produced 4GW of modules split evenly between Series 6 and Series 7. The company continues ramping domestic U.S. manufacturing, with its Louisiana facility expected to achieve commercial operations in H2 2025. The company addressed ongoing quality concerns with certain Series 7 modules, asserting appropriate corrective actions have been implemented into the production process. Regarding trade remedies, Widmar indicated the company remains prepared to pursue additional anti-dumping cases against countries where the data is supportive, including Indonesia, Laos, and Saudi Arabia.
